frameworks.name: Pavlovian control
frameworks.one_line: hardwired stereotyped responses to stimulus classes minimising computational cost

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: combined with drift-diffusion to capture invigoration/inhibition in choice and RT

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: captures RT-level effects of Pavlovian bias under aversive cues

core_question: How do aversive Pavlovian responses differ between escape and avoidance depending on proximity to an aversive state?
key_assumptions: Pavlovian control hardwires stereotyped responses to stimulus classes
Aversive proximity (escape vs avoidance) modulates activation vs inhibition
Choice and RT jointly index Pavlovian bias
manipulations: Proximity of aversive state (escape vs avoidance)
key_findings: Escape is associated with a bias toward vigorous action
Avoidance is associated with behavioral inhibition
Effects appear in both explicit choice and RT
interpretations: Aversive Pavlovian bias is activation vs inhibition depending on proximity
A combined RL + drift-diffusion model captures these invigoration/inhibition effects
